children.heading: Support activities
children.label: a Support activities
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture shall support the price of milk produced in the 48 contiguous States through the purchase of cheese, butter, and nonfat dry milk produced from the milk.
During each of calendar years 1999 through 2001, $9.90.
During the period beginning on , and ending on , $9.90.January 1, 2002May 31, 2002

children.heading: Purchase prices
children.label: c Purchase prices
children.text: The support purchase prices under this section for each of the products of milk (butter, cheese, and nonfat dry milk) announced by the Secretary shall be the same for all of that product sold by persons offering to sell the product to the Secretary. The purchase prices shall be sufficient to enable plants of average efficiency to pay producers, on average, a price that is not less than the rate of price support for milk in effect under subsection (b).

children.heading: Special rule for butter and nonfat dry milk purchase prices
children.label: d Special rule for butter and nonfat dry milk purchase prices

children.heading: Allocation of purchase prices
children.label: 1 Allocation of purchase prices
children.text: The Secretary may allocate the rate of price support between the purchase prices for nonfat dry milk and butter in a manner that will result in the lowest level of expenditures by the Commodity Credit Corporation or achieve such other objectives as the Secretary considers appropriate. Not later than 10 days after making or changing an allocation, the Secretary shall notify the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate of the allocation. shall not apply with respect to the implementation of this section.Section 553 of title 5

children.heading: Timing of purchase price adjustments
children.label: 2 Timing of purchase price adjustments
children.text: The Secretary may make any such adjustments in the purchase prices for nonfat dry milk and butter the Secretary considers to be necessary not more than twice in each calendar year.

children.heading: Refund required
children.label: 1 Refund required
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for a refund of the entire reduction required under section 204(h)(2) of the Agricultural Act of 1949 (), as in effect on the day before the amendment made by subsection (g), in the price of milk received by a producer during calendar year 1995 or 1996, if the producer provides evidence that the producer did not increase marketings in calendar year 1995 or 1996 when compared to calendar year 1994 or 1995, respectively.7 U.S.C. 1446e(h)(2)

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: This subsection shall not apply with respect to a producer for a particular calendar year if the producer has already received a refund under section 204(h) of the Agricultural Act of 1949 for the same fiscal year before the effective date of this section.

children.heading: Treatment of refund
children.label: 3 Treatment of refund
children.text: A refund under this subsection shall not be considered as any type of price support or payment for purposes of sections 3811 and 3821 of title 16.

children.heading: Commodity Credit Corporation
children.label: f Commodity Credit Corporation
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out the program authorized by this section through the Commodity Credit Corporation.

children.heading: Period of effectiveness
children.label: h Period of effectiveness
children.text: This section (other than subsection (g)) shall be effective only during the period beginning on the first day of the first month beginning after , and ending on . The program authorized by this section shall terminate on , and shall be considered to have expired notwithstanding .April 4, 1996May 31, 2002May 31, 2002section 907 of title 2
